desc: Knysna is the Garden Route's lagoon-and-forest hub, distinct from beachier Plettenberg Bay and larger George by combining oyster culture, indigenous woodland trails, and scenic Heads viewpoints in one compact base.
hls: Knysna Heads cliff viewpoints and lagoon cruises
Indigenous forest access unlike most coast-only Garden Route stops
Oyster-focused food scene with strong local wine pairing options
Well-positioned self-drive base between George, Plettenberg Bay, and Tsitsikamma

todo.name: Knysna Heads east + west lookouts
todo.desc: Visit both sides of the Heads for contrasting lagoon-mouth and Indian Ocean views.

todo.name: Featherbed Nature Reserve ferry and guided walk
todo.desc: Do the classic lagoon crossing plus reserve trail for iconic Knysna scenery.

todo.name: Jubilee Creek forest loop
todo.desc: Take the less-obvious high-value trail for giant yellowwoods, river pools, and quieter forest sections.

todo.name: Waterfront oyster tasting flight
todo.desc: Compare oysters from different farms with local Cap Classique or Sauvignon Blanc.

todo.name: Brenton-on-Sea sunset stop
todo.desc: Finish with dune viewpoints and a wide-sand beach walk west of town.

todo.name: Sedgefield Wild Oats market morning
todo.desc: Pair the Saturday market with nearby estuary boardwalks for a half-day side trip.

wifi.note: Strong in town properties; patchier in remote forest lodges.

prac.fltNote: From the UK/Ireland, the usual pattern is nonstop to Cape Town (CPT) or Johannesburg (JNB), then a domestic add-on to George (GRJ) plus a 1-1.5 hour road transfer; fully one-ticket routings exist but holiday periods (late Dec-Jan and Jul-Aug) can make domestic legs and car hire the bottleneck.

airports.name: Chief Dawid Stuurman International Airport (Port Elizabeth/Gqeberha)

prac.lang: English is widely used in tourism; Afrikaans and isiXhosa are also common in the Knysna area.
prac.tz: SAST (UTC+2) year-round; no daylight saving time.
prac.visa: South Africa entry is nationality-based: some passports are visa-exempt for short stays, while others need a visa in advance and may face strict document checks.
alerts: Strong summer southeasterly winds can cancel lagoon and ocean departures at short notice.
Wildfire risk rises during hot berg-wind spells; monitor SANParks and provincial road advisories before forest drives.
N2 crash or roadwork delays are common in holiday weeks, so avoid same-day tight airport check-in plans.

sweet: Oct-Nov and Mar-May for mild weather and better value than peak South African summer.
